risk_description: Model trained only on disbursed loans — no ground truth for rejected applicants. Model has never observed whether its own rejections were correct creating selection bias in training population.
control_description: Annual reject inference study; document limitation in model card; human review for borderline cases

risk_description: Model trained on historical data that may not reflect current MSME credit behaviour — economic cycles post-COVID and rate changes may have shifted default patterns significantly.
control_description: PSI monitoring dashboard; quarterly outcome validation; defined retraining triggers

risk_description: Quality of bureau GST and banking data inputs may degrade in production — missing values out-of-range inputs or stale bureau pulls could corrupt model inputs without detection.
control_description: Great Expectations validation suite (94.3% recall); DQ scorecard monitoring; input validation at API layer

risk_description: Processing of personal financial data for credit scoring requires valid consent under DPDP Act 2023. Current privacy policy references withdrawn PDP Bill 2021 — material compliance gap.
control_description: Consent management system built (Module 3); privacy policy update required; DPO appointment needed

risk_description: Transformation steps from raw bureau/GST/banking data to model features are not publicly documented — regulators and auditors cannot trace how features are derived from source data.
control_description: Data catalogue and lineage map built (Module 2); Mermaid lineage diagram; 6 assets documented

risk_description: Model recall on default class is 0.59 — 41% of actual defaults are predicted as safe and approved. At average MSME loan sizes this represents material expected credit loss.
control_description: Scale_pos_weight=3.6 applied; threshold tuning options documented; outcome monitoring required

risk_description: Predicted default probabilities systematically over-estimate risk for Female proprietors northern states and Food Processing sector relative to actual observed default rates.
control_description: Bias audit complete (Module 5); fairness-constrained retraining planned; threshold adjustment interim measure
regulatory_reference: RBI Fair Practices Code; EU AI Act Article 10

risk_description: Last independent model validation was June 2024. Three open findings including one Critical (algorithmic bias). Next validation due June 2025.
control_description: Model inventory tracks validation schedule; MRF-001/002/003 findings documented

risk_description: No publicly documented policy for model versioning rollback procedures or change management for model updates. Risk of uncontrolled model changes in production.
control_description: Model saved in XGBoost JSON format; feature names versioned; git-based version control

risk_description: XGBoost models with large feature sets are susceptible to overfitting — appearing to perform well on training data while failing on out-of-distribution production inputs.
control_description: Train/test split stratified 80/20; AUC evaluated on held-out test set; moderate hyperparameters deliberately chosen

risk_description: Model produces higher predicted default rates for Female proprietors than actual default rates justify. Equalized Odds Difference of 0.172 breaches 0.10 threshold. Four-fifths rule breached.
control_description: Interim threshold adjustment; human review mandate for borderline cases; fairness-constrained retraining planned
regulatory_reference: RBI Fair Practices Code; DPDP Act 2023; EU AI Act Article 10

risk_description: Equalized Odds Difference of 0.446 across states — most severe finding in audit. Delhi: 10.1% actual default but 23.9% predicted. 6 of 10 states breach four-fifths rule.
control_description: State-level approval rate monitoring; Delhi retrospective review; state dummy replacement with economic indicators planned
regulatory_reference: RBI Fair Practices Code; RBI Priority Sector Lending; Constitution Art 15/16

risk_description: Food Processing sector: 13.1% actual default but 29.7% predicted — 16.6pp over-prediction gap. EOD of 0.225 across sectors. Auto Components under-predicted.
control_description: Food Processing retrospective review; sector dummy replacement planned; sector monitoring added to dashboard
regulatory_reference: RBI Fair Practices Code; Constitution Art 15/16

risk_description: SHAP analysis confirms bias mechanism is distributional confounding not direct discrimination. Sector and state features proxy for gender/community correlations in training data. Removing protected attributes insufficient.
control_description: Proxy discrimination assessment planned; training data re-weighting; community correlation study commissioned
regulatory_reference: EU AI Act Article 10; Constitution Art 15/16

risk_title: Chouldechova Impossibility — No Single Fairness Solution
risk_description: Mathematical impossibility of simultaneously satisfying demographic parity equalized odds and individual fairness when base rates differ. Board must make explicit policy decision on which criterion to prioritise.
control_description: Impossibility theorem documented in bias audit report; Board policy decision required; legal review initiated
regulatory_reference: NIST AI RMF GV-1; ISO 42001 Clause 6.1

risk_description: Population Stability Index for dpd_90_count_12m reached 0.220 under simulated production drift — above 0.20 critical threshold. Model review triggered.
control_description: PSI monitoring dashboard operational (Module 4); monthly automated alerts; retraining triggers defined

risk_description: Rejected MSME applicants cannot currently receive an explanation of why their application was rejected. DPDP Act and RBI Fair Practices Code both support right to explanation.
control_description: SHAP-based rejection letter process planned; top-3 SHAP factors to be included in rejection communication
regulatory_reference: DPDP Act 2023; RBI Fair Practices Code

risk_description: Fully automated credit decisions with no documented human override mechanism for borderline cases (predicted probability 0.35–0.65). EU AI Act Article 14 mandates human oversight for High Risk AI.
control_description: Human review mandate planned for borderline cases; escalation procedure to be documented
regulatory_reference: EU AI Act Article 14; RBI Digital Lending Guidelines

risk_description: No publicly documented procedure for responding to AI failures — model producing incorrect outputs at scale discriminatory outcomes or regulatory breach. Response time and escalation path undefined.
control_description: AI incident response procedure developed (Module 6); P1-P4 classification; 4-hour P1 response target
regulatory_reference: NIST AI RMF MG-4; ISO 42001 Clause 10

risk_description: No publicly documented pre-deployment checklist requiring bias testing validation sign-off and compliance review before a new model version goes live.
control_description: Model inventory tracks deployment; model card documents limitations; governance checklist recommended

risk_description: UGRO Capital's public privacy policy references PDP Bill 2021 which was withdrawn. Enacted legislation is DPDP Act 2023. This is a citable public compliance gap.
control_description: Gap analysis complete (Module 3); 15 gaps identified — 4 critical; remediation plan documented

risk_title: RBI Fair Practices Code — Discrimination Risk
risk_description: Confirmed bias findings (gender EOD 0.172; state EOD 0.446; sector EOD 0.225) create material risk of breaching RBI Fair Practices Code prohibition on discriminatory lending.
control_description: Bias audit complete; mitigation roadmap with 16 actions; Board notification required
regulatory_reference: RBI Fair Practices Code; RBI Digital Lending Guidelines 2022

risk_title: EU AI Act High Risk Non-Conformity
risk_description: Credit scoring classified as High Risk AI under EU AI Act Annex III. Requires conformity assessment transparency obligations and human oversight. Current implementation lacks several required elements.
control_description: Model card documents EU AI Act classification; human oversight gap identified; conformity assessment not completed

risk_description: Bureau and GST data processed for credit scoring — unclear whether any processing involves cross-border transfer which would require DPDP Act Chapter III compliance.
control_description: Data lineage map identifies source systems; cross-border transfer assessment recommended

risk_description: DPDP Rules 2025 still being finalised. RBI AI guidance evolving. MeitY AI framework under development. Regulatory landscape for AI in NBFC lending may change materially within 12 months.
control_description: Regulatory monitoring process recommended; quarterly regulatory horizon scan

risk_description: If algorithmic bias findings (CRITICAL across all 3 protected attributes) were publicly disclosed — via regulatory action media investigation or whistleblower — reputational damage would be significant for a self-described DataTech NBFC.
control_description: Bias remediation underway; proactive regulatory engagement recommended; communications plan advised

risk_description: RBI enforcement action for Fair Practices Code breach would be publicly disclosed. DPDP Act enforcement by Data Protection Board carries financial penalties and public naming.
control_description: Compliance remediation programme in progress; legal review initiated; regulatory engagement recommended
regulatory_reference: DPDP Act 2023 Section 33; RBI Enforcement Policy

risk_description: UGRO Capital's mission includes financial inclusion for MSMEs. Systematic under-approval in UP WB MP (large MSME states) and for Female proprietors directly contradicts this stated mission.
control_description: Geographic and gender bias remediation planned; financial inclusion metrics to be added to model governance dashboard
regulatory_reference: RBI Priority Sector Lending; SIDBI MSME mandate

risk_description: Institutional investors increasingly assess AI governance as part of ESG evaluation. CRITICAL bias findings and DPDP compliance gap may affect ESG ratings and investor sentiment.
control_description: ESG disclosure of AI governance improvements recommended; Responsible AI Policy publication advised

risk_description: Public disclosure of 25000+ features in GRO Score 3.0 creates significant interpretability challenges. Regulators and borrowers cannot meaningfully understand decisions driven by thousands of features.
control_description: SHAP explainability implemented; top features identified; rejection explanation process planned; feature importance documented
regulatory_reference: EU AI Act Article 13; RBI Fair Practices Code

risk_description: Heavy reliance on CIBIL/Experian bureau data (bureau_score is top SHAP feature at 1.131) creates single-point-of-failure risk — bureau data outage or quality degradation directly impacts model performance.
control_description: Multi-bureau data strategy recommended; fallback scoring rules for bureau-unavailable cases; SLA monitoring

risk_description: Model rejections reduce the pool of borrowers from disadvantaged groups — future training data contains even fewer Female/northern-state/Food Processing borrowers — amplifying existing bias in next model version.
control_description: Reject inference study planned; training data re-weighting; fairness constraints at retraining; diverse training data strategy
regulatory_reference: EU AI Act Article 10; NIST AI RMF MP-2
